text:
- **[NMP #2865](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2865)** — Phase 0 subissue: bring `nmp-nip60` into workspace/CI, capability-gate the false `pay_invoice` stubs, demote the legacy `kind:17375` relay-tag authority. [...]     "summary": "Re-adding nmp-nip60 to workspace.members now compiles a mandatory ureq TLS HTTP client + 409-line synchronous Cashu mint client (src/cashu/client.rs) into every `cargo build`/`cargo test --workspace`/CI run, even though the PR's own docs redefine the crate scope to 'NIP mechanics only — event codecs, types, pure shape validation' with all networking in the not-yet-existent nmp-wallet. Sibling HTTP-using crates (nmp-nip57, nmp-nip05) gate ureq behind an optional `native` feature (`native = [\"dep:ureq\"]`); nmp-nip60 makes it unconditional. Cheaper: gate ureq + cashu::client behind an optional feature, or move client.rs to nmp-wallet, so builds that do not exercise minting skip ureq + rustls/ring compilation.", [...] Removed-behavior audit for PR #2866. The two headline concerns you flagged both clear: WalletConfig is NOT serde-derived (the kind:17375 content is a hand-built JSON [key,value] pair array; `relay` lives in event *tags*, not content), so `relays`→`legacy_relay_hint` is a pure Rust field rename with zero effect on serialized/persisted/wire shape — NIP-60 round-trip is unchanged. And the removed WalletBackend/PayResult/WalletError trait + re-exports have NO consumers anywhere in the checkout (only self-references and unrelated nmp-nwc `pay_invoice`), so nothing downstream breaks. Ownership.rs's 7 `exclusive:true` kind claims (17375/7375/7376/7374/10019/9321/38172) have no colliding claim in any other crate's ownership.rs, and the D26 protocol-crate test already listed nmp-nip60. Genuinely thin. Two low-confidence candidates below.

[
  {
    "file": "crates/nmp-nip60/src/nip60_wallet/nutzap_send.rs",
    "line": 84,
    "summary": "publish_nutzap_info still writes legacy_relay_hint into the kind:10019 relays field the diff's own docs declare non-authoritative",
    "failure_scenario": "Every doc comment touched by this PR (lib.rs, mint_announce.rs, wallet_event.rs, nip60_wallet.rs, and the legacy_relay_hint() accessor) newly and emphatically asserts the kind:17375 relay tags are legacy residue that 'must never' be used as relay-selection source of truth — the authoritative set is kind:10019/NIP-65. But publish_nutzap_info still populates the *published* kind:10019 NutZap-info `relays` straight from self.legacy_relay_hint. kind:10019 relays are the ONE place NIP-61 treats this data as authoritative: senders read the recipient's kind:10019 relays to know where to publish kind:9321 nutzaps. A wallet decoded via from_wallet_event from a kind:17375 that had no relay tags gets an empty legacy_relay_hint, so publish_nutzap_info emits a kind:10019 advertising an empty relay set, and inbound nutzaps become undeliverable — the user silently receives nothing. The behavior is unchanged from before the rename (pre-existing quirk, not a code regression), but the PR's new contract now directly contradicts this write path, so it's the highest-value thing to flag." [...]     "summary": "The same 'the kind:17375 relay tags are a legacy, non-authoritative hint; kind:10019/NIP-65 is the source of truth; nmp-wallet owns relay policy; see nip60-nip61-wallet-design.md' paragraph is restated ~7 times across this PR: lib.rs:22-27, mint_announce.rs:11-16, nip60_wallet.rs module+field+accessor, wallet_event.rs module+field, nutzap_send.rs:74-79. Simpler: state it once (e.g. on the accessor) and link the other sites to it.", [...]     "summary": "Rename is skin-deep on the WRITE path: freshly-built kind:17375 events still emit `relay` tags from `legacy_relay_hint`. The design doc's Relay Acquisition section sanctions only PARSING legacy tags ('parse it only as a non-authoritative compatibility hint'), i.e. read-compat for old wallets — not emitting new ones. `WalletConfig::generate(mints, legacy_relay_hint)` + `build_wallet_event` keep producing the exact residue activation was supposed to stop creating. Deeper fix: new kind:17375 should carry no relay tags (nmp-wallet owns relay acquisition); the constructor should not take a relay-hint param at all.",
    "failure_scenario": "Every wallet created after this PR still bakes relay tags into its kind:17375. When nmp-wallet lands and treats kind:10019/NIP-65 as authoritative, these self-emitted 17375 tags become live legacy data forever, so the 'legacy' hint is perpetuated by new writes rather than being a finite decode-only compat surface — defeating the purpose of the rename." [...]     "summary": "`publish_nutzap_info` hardcodes `self.legacy_relay_hint` as the published kind:10019 `relay` tags, yet its own new doc comment (lines 74-79) says callers 'should prefer publishing' the authoritative kind:10019/NIP-65 set — but the method takes no parameter to pass one. The documented-correct path is unreachable through this API.",
    "failure_scenario": "The helper either silently publishes the non-authoritative legacy relays (the exact behavior the rest of this PR's docs warn against), or every caller must bypass the helper and rebuild the kind:10019 event by hand — defeating its purpose. The advisory doc describes a capability the signature doesn't provide, so it reads as guidance no one can follow." [...]     "failure_scenario": "The PR renames the field to legacy_relay_hint and adds doc comments everywhere insisting it 'must never be treated as the relay-selection source of truth' — the authoritative set is the user's kind:10019 relay tags. But publish_nutzap_info still sets `relays: self.legacy_relay_hint.clone()` on the kind:10019 event it publishes. legacy_relay_hint is populated from the wallet's kind:17375 `relay` tags (from_wallet_event). So the legacy hint is copied verbatim INTO the authoritative kind:10019 surface. Two concrete failures: (a) if the old kind:17375 carried stale relay tags, they are re-minted as the authoritative kind:10019 relay set, so nutzap senders resolving the recipient's kind:10019 will publish kind:9321 nutzaps to relays the recipient no longer reads and the recipient never sees/redeems them; (b) in the going-forward common case where kind:17375 has no relay tags, legacy_relay_hint is empty, so the published kind:10019 has zero relay tags — degrading recipients to NIP-65 fallback and losing wallet-scoped relay hints entirely. The docstring acknowledges this as an intentional default, so it may be by-design, but it directly contradicts the PR's stated 'drop legacy 17375 relay-tag authority' goal and is the strongest correctness/semantics candidate in the diff." [...]     "failure_scenario": "decode_wallet_event reads kind:17375 `relay` tags into legacy_relay_hint; build_wallet_event writes config.legacy_relay_hint back out as `relay` tags. So any decode->rebuild->republish round-trip (e.g. adding a mint and re-signing the wallet event) re-stamps the legacy relay tags onto the new kind:17375. This keeps the 'legacy design residue' alive indefinitely rather than letting it age out, and means downstream tooling that still (wrongly) reads kind:17375 relay tags keeps getting fed them. Low confidence / likely intentional round-trip fidelity, but it works against the PR's stated intent to deprecate the 17375 relay-tag surface."
